audit: pzoom static-analysis findings on the Psalm codebase

analyzer.branch: claude/dreamy-wright-na9bh5 (rebased on main thru #42; pzoom parity fixes landed in #41 and #42)

methodology: Ran the release pzoom binary on the Psalm project (vendor installed via composer; Psalm's own psalm-baseline.xml applied, as pzoom does automatically). Ground truth for 'what Psalm itself reports' was obtained by running Psalm on its own codebase with --ignore-baseline (1658 findings, almost all otherwise baselined). Each pzoom issue was compared against Psalm's findings and verified by reading the PHP source.

classification_legend.false_positive: pzoom is wrong: no real problem, or a pzoom analysis/stub/parsing/suppression-scope bug. Psalm correctly stays silent.
classification_legend.false_negative: the visible issue is a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress, but it is the symptom of a pzoom false NEGATIVE: pzoom fails to emit the underlying suppressed check (which Psalm does emit) and therefore thinks the @psalm-suppress is unused. The fix is to implement the missing check.
classification_legend.true_issue_psalm_misses: pzoom correctly found a genuine issue in the PHP code that Psalm does NOT report (pzoom is more precise here).
classification_legend.real_but_psalm_suppresses_or_baselines: pzoom's finding is correct AND Psalm's own analysis produces it too, but Psalm intentionally suppresses it inline or via psalm-baseline.xml; pzoom surfaces it only because it does not honor that suppression/baseline entry (a suppression/baseline-matching gap, not an analysis error).
classification_legend.approved_pzoom_divergence: a known, accepted divergence that is NOT a bug to fix. ComplexMethod: pzoom's data-flow graph is structurally denser than Psalm's variable-use graph, so its complexity metric is calibrated to its own graph and flags a different (but reasonable) set of methods than Psalm. Matching Psalm's exact set would require reimplementing Psalm's graph. Return-type prototype: pzoom reports return mismatches per statement and no longer emits the function-level MoreSpecificReturnType / InvalidNullableReturnType / InvalidFalsableReturnType (see README 'Differences to Psalm'); a now-unused @psalm-suppress MoreSpecificReturnType therefore surfaces as UnusedPsalmSuppress.

count_note: 62 issues. Breakdown: 2 false_positive, 39 false_negative (each a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress whose underlying check pzoom does not yet emit), 7 true_issue_psalm_misses (write-only static properties), 5 real_but_psalm_suppresses_or_baselines (3 baselined PossiblyUndefinedIntArrayOffset + 1 baselined UnusedPsalmSuppress + 1 LessSpecificReturnStatement in UnionTrait that Psalm baselines as MoreSpecificReturnType), 9 approved_pzoom_divergence (8 ComplexMethod + 1 UnusedPsalmSuppress from the return-type prototype dropping MoreSpecificReturnType). The 2 remaining false positives -- key() non-emptiness through array_keys/unset/count (Reconciler.php) and a self:: static-property read in AsyncTestCase subclasses -- are deferred as subtle. The return-type prototype (per-statement return diagnostics; see README) adds the 2 trait-return entries above. Psalm's composer.lock is git-ignored so vendor versions float.

false_negative_root_causes.ArgumentTypeCoercion (3): 2 AlgebraTest complex Clause array-shape args (devs annotate 'due to Psalm bug') and 1 Scanner static-method-call arg -- pzoom does not emit the string->lowercase-string coercion there.
false_negative_root_causes.InvalidArrayOffset (3): `$break_types[count($break_types) - $count]` -- a computed int offset that may be negative/out-of-range on a list. pzoom does no int-range validation of computed offsets.
false_negative_root_causes.MoreSpecificReturnType (3) + LessSpecifi: Deeply-nested specific docblock return types (e.g. list<non-empty-array<string, non-empty-list<non-empty-list<Assertion>>>>). pzoom infers the return more loosely and does not compare declared-vs-inferred specificity through nested generics, so it emits neither the MoreSpecific nor the paired LessSpecific. Same methods produce both.
false_negative_root_causes.ImpureFunctionCall (1): array_walk_recursive() with a by-ref-writing closure -- the closure should be inferred impure and propagated to the call, which needs deeper closure-purity work.
false_negative_root_causes.RiskyTruthyFalsyComparison (3): `file_get_contents() ?: ''` and `match($mode)` on a string that could be the falsy '0'. pzoom's RiskyTruthyFalsyComparison does not fire for a string-that-could-be-'0' (or int|string) in a ?:/match truthy context.
false_negative_root_causes.MixedAssignment (3): `$seconds += $this->timeLimit` and `foreach ($value as &$type)` where the source is mixed. pzoom does not propagate mixed into a compound-assign / by-ref foreach element target.
false_negative_root_causes.RedundantCondition (3): instanceof / in_array conditions Psalm proves always-true/false from inferred types; pzoom's redundant-condition detection misses them (one references vimeo/psalm#10296).
false_negative_root_causes.long_tail (17): PossiblyInvalidArrayAccess 2, MixedOperand 2, and 1 each of PossiblyFalseArgument, MixedArrayAccess, MixedArgumentTypeCoercion, InvalidArgument, UnusedParam, PropertyTypeCoercion, ImpureStaticProperty, PossiblyNullPropertyFetch, PossiblyNullArgument, MutableDependency, TypeDoesNotContainType, ReferenceConstraintViolation, InvalidFalsableReturnType -- each a single under-emission in the same families (purity, mixed-propagation, nullable-chains, narrowing coercion, specificity).

true_issue_psalm_misses_detail: 7 PossiblyUnusedProperty/UnusedProperty on write-only STATIC properties ($config x6, IssueBuffer::$console_issues): genuine dead storage where pzoom is more precise than Psalm (confirmed by the project author); Psalm reports neither.

false_positive_root_causes.imprecise_array_non_emptiness_tracking (: `(string) key($reference_graph[$references_to_fix[0]])`: in isolation pzoom and Psalm both type key(array<string,true>) as string|null. pzoom over-narrows the inner array to non-empty through the surrounding array_keys()/count()>1/unset() operations, so key() loses its null and the (string) cast looks redundant. An array non-emptiness tracking divergence -- deferred as subtle.
false_positive_root_causes.incomplete_reference_tracking_unused_pro: AsyncTestCase::$src_dir_path is read via self::$src_dir_path in subclasses (TaintTest.php etc.); pzoom misses that read for a re-declared static property, so it reports the property unused. Psalm tracks it as used.

issues.message: Suppression of PossiblyInvalidArrayAccess is never used
issues.explanation: False negative (manifesting as a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress): the `@psalm-suppress PossiblyInvalidArrayAccess` is genuinely required — Psalm emits PossiblyInvalidArrayAccess here and the suppression consumes it (Psalm's findUnusedPsalmSuppress flags only ONE unused suppression codebase-wide, at Cli/Psalm.php:1071, so every other suppression is in use). pzoom does NOT emit PossiblyInvalidArrayAccess at this spot, so it wrongly concludes the suppression is unused. The underlying defect is that pzoom should emit PossiblyInvalidArrayAccess here but does not; closing that gap would both surface PossiblyInvalidArrayAccess and make this UnusedPsalmSuppress disappear.

issues.message: Suppression of ComplexMethod is never used
issues.explanation: Approved pzoom divergence: pzoom's data-flow graph is denser than Psalm's variable-use graph, so its hand-calibrated complexity limit (graph size 800 vs Psalm's 200) flags a different set of methods. Here pzoom does not consider the method complex, so the `@psalm-suppress ComplexMethod` looks unused. Accepted: matching Psalm's exact set needs Psalm's graph construction, not a constant tweak.

issues.message: This method’s complexity is greater than the project limit (method graph size = 874, average path length = 118)
issues.explanation: pzoom's method-complexity metric (graph size / path length) diverges from Psalm's. Psalm flags Config.php:927 and ArgumentAnalyzer.php:712 (both baselined) and does NOT flag this method; pzoom crosses the limit here instead. Not a logic defect.

issues.message: Possibly undefined array offset '1' is risky given expected type 'int'. Consider using isset beforehand.
issues.explanation: Psalm emits this same PossiblyUndefinedIntArrayOffset and it is covered by psalm-baseline.xml. pzoom emits it because its extracted snippet text does not match the baseline <code> entry, so the baseline filter misses it. The underlying finding matches Psalm.

issues.message: This method’s complexity is greater than the project limit (method graph size = 831, average path length = 145)

issues.explanation: pzoom's complexity calculation diverges from Psalm's; Psalm does not flag this method. Metric divergence, not a code defect.

issues.message: Suppression of RedundantCondition is never used
issues.explanation: False negative (manifesting as a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress): the `@psalm-suppress RedundantCondition` is genuinely required — Psalm emits RedundantCondition here and the suppression consumes it (Psalm's findUnusedPsalmSuppress flags only ONE unused suppression codebase-wide, at Cli/Psalm.php:1071, so every other suppression is in use). pzoom does NOT emit RedundantCondition at this spot, so it wrongly concludes the suppression is unused. The underlying defect is that pzoom should emit RedundantCondition here but does not; closing that gap would both surface RedundantCondition and make this UnusedPsalmSuppress disappear.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to private property Psalm\IssueBuffer::$console_issues
issues.explanation: Good divergence: private static $console_issues is written via `self::$console_issues = []` (line 1027) and never read — genuine dead storage pzoom rightly surfaces. Psalm does not flag write-only static properties.

issues.message: Redundant cast to string given docblock-provided type
issues.explanation: `(string) key($reference_graph[$references_to_fix[0]])`. In isolation pzoom and Psalm both type key(array<string,true>) as string|null (no RedundantCast). The FP comes from pzoom over-narrowing $reference_graph[$references_to_fix[0]] to a non-empty array through the surrounding array_keys()/count()>1/unset() operations, so key() loses its null and the cast looks redundant. An array non-emptiness tracking divergence, not a key() modelling gap -- deferred as subtle/risky.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to property Psalm\Tests\AsyncTestCase::$src_dir_path
issues.explanation: Real FP (NOT a good divergence): $src_dir_path is genuinely read via `self::$src_dir_path` in AsyncTestCase subclasses (TaintTest.php:31, etc.). pzoom misses the read for this re-declared static property; Psalm correctly treats it as used.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to property Psalm\Tests\Config\ConfigTest::$config
issues.explanation: Good divergence: genuine dead storage that pzoom rightly surfaces and Psalm misses. `$config` is a static property written via `self::$config = new TestConfig()` in setUp and never read anywhere (only the `new TestConfig()` side effect matters; the assignment target is unused). Psalm treats any static-property access as a use and stays silent; pzoom correctly reports the property as unused.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to property Psalm\Tests\Config\Plugin\EventHandler\AddTaints\AddTaintsInterfaceTest::$config
issues.explanation: Good divergence: write-only static $config (assigned `new TestConfig()` in setUp, never read). Pzoom rightly flags the dead storage; Psalm misses it (treats any static access as a use).

issues.message: Suppression of MoreSpecificReturnType is never used
issues.explanation: Expected consequence of the return-type prototype: pzoom no longer emits MoreSpecificReturnType (return mismatches are reported per statement instead; see the README 'Differences to Psalm'), so the `@psalm-suppress MoreSpecificReturnType` here is now unused. Psalm still emits MoreSpecificReturnType and the suppression consumes it; this is a deliberate, accepted divergence, not a bug to fix.

issues.message: The type Psalm\Type\Atomic does not match the declared return type Psalm\Type\Atomic\TArray|Psalm\Type\Atomic\TClassStringMap|Psalm\Type\Atomic\TKeyedArray
issues.explanation: Real return-type looseness: UnionTrait::getArray returns $this->types['array'] (Atomic) against the narrower docblock TArray|TKeyedArray|TClassStringMap. Psalm detects it as MoreSpecificReturnType and baselines it (psalm-baseline.xml, UnionTrait.php). pzoom reports the paired per-statement LessSpecificReturnStatement -- the return-type prototype runs the per-`return` checks inside trait bodies, where Psalm guards them -- and surfaces it because it does not apply Psalm's psalm-baseline.xml entry.
